title: HELM Task Capability Token v1
description: Short-lived, task-bound authorization token implementing grant-on-demand / auto-revoke least privilege (docs/governance/task-capability-tokens.md). The guardian refuses dispatch for expired, exhausted, out-of-scope, or revoked tokens. Status: preview — schema only.

task_id.description: Stable identifier of the task/intent this token is bound to. Dispatch under any other task_id must fail closed.

manifest_hash.description: Hash of the exact manifest revision granted. Manifest drift invalidates the token (fail closed).

expires_at.description: TTL boundary. Recommended default: task end or 15 minutes, whichever is earlier.

max_uses.description: Use-count ceiling for single-shot or bounded grants. Absent means bounded only by expiry.

args_digest.description: When present, dispatch arguments must canonical-hash to this digest (exact-argument grant).

data_boundary_ceiling.description: Grant is void if the dispatch would exceed this data boundary, even if the manifest allows more.

status.description: Terminal states are fail-closed: no dispatch, receipt written for the refusal.

revocation_receipt_ref.description: Receipt recording when and why the token was revoked (task end, emergency stop, scope violation).

signature.description: Detached signature over the canonical token payload, verifiable offline like other HELM receipts.
